topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a familiar, jarring sound cutting through the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched. I stared at the name displayed: Mark. Mark, the kid who used to make my stomach clench just thinking about school. A friend request. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. I hadn't expected this. A wave of unfamiliar heat rushed up my neck, making the fine hairs on my arms prickle. I slowly navigated to his profile, my eyes scanning the photos—old, awkward snapshots from elementary school. A slow, almost imperceptible shake ran through my shoulders. I didn't know what to do with this unexpected digital intrusion.

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug when my phone chimed. It was a request from Mark. My hand froze halfway to the keyboard. For a moment, the world narrowed down to the glowing rectangle in my palm. My heart began to thump a rapid, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I took a slow, deliberate sip of the lukewarm liquid, trying to anchor myself. Why him? The thought surfaced, sharp and unwelcome. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and reread the message prompt. My gaze drifted to the screen again, a strange tension tightening my jaw. I didn't move to accept it immediately.

[story 3]

When the alert popped up, I stopped mid-sentence, my pen hovering over the blank page. The suddenness of it hit me like a physical shove. I blinked hard, trying to process the screen. Mark. The name flashed there, accompanied by a request to connect. A strange stillness settled over me, a sudden vacuum where my usual forward momentum used to be. I felt a distinct lightness in my chest, coupled with a knot forming low in my stomach. I picked up my phone, turning it over in my hands as if examining a foreign object. I hesitated, my thumb hovering over the acceptance button, a visible hesitation etched across my features.
